title: stelis canae
intro: Stelis canae, commonly known as the stelis orchid, is a plant species belonging to the orchid family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The therapeutic actions of Stelis canae include its potential to act as an antimicrobial agent, a diuretic, and an anti-hypertensive, making it a promising treatment for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Stelis canae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
constituents: Stelis canae has many active constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These constituents, including compounds like kaempferol, quercetin, and isorhapontigenin, possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, improve heart health, and boost the immune system. The flavonoids present in Stelis canae, such as myricetin and fisetin, have been shown to have anti-cancer properties, while the alkaloids, including stelisin and stelisine, can help lower blood pressure and improve cognitive function. By harnessing the medicinal potential of Stelis canae, individuals can potentially lead healthier lives, experiencing improved overall well-being and reduced risk of chronic diseases.
side_effects: Stelis canae can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and stomach upset due to its toxic compounds. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can lead to vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain. Prolonged exposure to the plant's toxins can also cause liver and kidney damage, which may be irreversible in severe cases. Improper use of Stelis canae can also trigger allergic reactions, including skin rashes and respiratory issues in susceptible individuals.
